risinfo.chaptertitle: Appendix II. Test Your Knowledge! Answer Key

sect1.title: Chapter 11
sect1.para: 1. c. None. Modifiers 26 and TC are not appended because 93010 designate that only the professional component (interpretation and report) was provided. Codes 93000 and 93005 are used to report the global service or technical component, respectively. A reduced service modifier 52 is not indicated because the full service was completed.

2. d. Report collection of blood samples for testing (eg, venipuncture) in a physician practice regardless of whether the laboratory test is performed in the office or at an outside facility. Other specimen collection (eg, nasal or oral swab, urine collection) is typically not separately reported. Code 51701 is reported when a non-indwelling bladder catheter (straight catheterization) is inserted for a urine culture collection.

3. d. Laboratories and physician offices performing waived tests should append modifier QW to the CPT code for CLIA-waived procedures. Only a small number of CLIA-waived tests are exempt from use of modifier QW (eg, 81002, non-automated urinalysis without microscopy; 81025, urine pregnancy test; 82272, occult blood by peroxidase for other than colorectal neoplasm screening).
4. c. Codes for proprietary laboratory analyses or multianalyte assays with algorithmic analyses are selected on the basis of the analysis performed and the proprietary name of the test.

5. d. All of the above. The physician's or QHP's analysis of test results that do not require interpretation and report is part of an E/M service. Time spent providing a separately reported testing service does not contribute to the total time of an E/M service. Modifier 25 may be required on the E/M service to indicate the service was significant and is separately identifiable in the documentation.
